title: rosa eglanteria var. lutea
intro: Rosa eglanteria var. lutea, also known as the yellow dog rose or yellow briar, is a flowering plant species that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant are numerous, including its ability to aid in digestion, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in herbal infusions and as a poultice for topical applications.
